overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing primarily on research related to computational social science, disinformation, and information operations. LinkedIn has the largest follower base, while Bluesky shows active engagement with recent research publications and conference participation. Overall engagement is solid, with LinkedIn posts generating more comments than other platforms.

contentThemes.description: Research, methods, and analysis related to computational approaches to social phenomena.
Studies and discussions related to the spread of disinformation and coordinated information operations.
Posts related to grant awards, hiring postdoctoral researchers, and opportunities for PhD students.

recommendations: Increase cross-platform promotion to drive followers to different platforms.
Experiment with different content formats (e.g., videos, infographics) to boost engagement.
Engage more actively in conversations by responding to comments and questions.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky is effectively used to share research publications and conference presentations, showing strong engagement around academic content.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon is used to share updates on research tools and engage in discussions about methodologies, although engagement is relatively lower compared to other platforms.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn is a key platform for announcing research grants and hiring opportunities, with high engagement through likes and comments.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
